title: Vibe Calibration: Autonomous Bring-up of a 112-Qubit Superconducting Quantum Processor by a Skill-Orchestrating Language Agent
acquisition_purpose: Primary-source acquisition for queued review-vibe-calibration-tacit-skill-transfer-validity; this record does not claim a deep review.

paper.withdrawal_status: The immutable v1 API summary contains no withdrawal or retraction notice.
paper.extraction_status: Full immutable 32-page v1 PDF extracted successfully. The title and abstract, three-phase method, Skill decision tree and quantitative gates, fine-tuning data, physical-system results, transfer discussion, supplement, transcripts, references, and final physical PDF page were inspected for continuity.

official_release.paper_link_evidence: The immutable paper cites the NVIDIA Quantum Calibration Agent Blueprint URL as reference 22 and states an access date of 2026-05-29. The queued review specifies the exact commit below.
official_release.paper_release_timing: The pinned commit predates arXiv v1 (2026-06-21) and the paper's stated repository access date (2026-05-29). It is a timing-bounded snapshot, not proof of the exact state inspected by the authors on the access date.
official_release.inspectable_artifacts: The pinned archive contains the Apache-2.0 reference blueprint, provider-agnostic agent runner, experiment and workflow interfaces, example quantum-measurement scripts, six generic SKILL.md files, documentation, UI, and unit/integration tests.
official_release.correspondence_boundary: A static text search of the extracted Markdown files found no Vibe Calibration title, arXiv ID, 112-qubit result, tacit-distillation terminology, validated-trajectory terminology, or paper-specific decision-tree text. The archive is locally inspectable as the cited NVIDIA reference blueprint, but acquisition alone does not establish that it contains the paper's private Skill library, training trajectories, fine-tuned checkpoints, physical measurements, audit records, or exact experimental implementation.

claim_boundary: This acquisition establishes local, hash-pinned paper and release evidence only. It is not a deep review and does not validate faithful tacit-skill transfer, threshold authority, expert equivalence, calibrated physical correctness, autonomous failure recovery, efficiency, cross-device transport, safety, general capability, production fitness, or readiness.
